events.title: Mini Horse Mania
events.description:
Join us on April 25th, 2026, from 10:00 am until 12:00 pm for a FREE event: Mini Horse Mania! 
First, visit the “adoption barn” and learn all about how firefighters cared for fire horses. Then pick your own stuffed horse to adopt and call your own! Finally, all kids will have the opportunity to meet our guests: mini horses! Take this opportunity to get to know and show your love to these cuties and come horse around with us at the Museum!
All activities are FREE and will be outdoors (tents will be used in the event of rain). Free admission to the Museum will also be offered to all who attend this special event. Reservations are not required for this event.
Our special thanks to High & Mighty Therapeutic Riding and Driving Center, Inc., for partnering with us to present this program. Learn more about the great work High & Mighty does at: www.high-n-mighty.org

events.title: The Chancellor’s Sheep and Wool Showcase 2026
events.description:
The rolling hills and river views at Clermont State Historic Site will be alive with the sound of music, the delicious aroma of hot food, and the brilliant yarns, soaps, and artisanal goods by local makers.

​

This family-friendly event, one of the historic site’s oldest and most popular annual festivals, has become a springtime tradition for many in the area. With sheering demonstrations, farm animals, and historic reenactments, there’s something for everyone! The Showcase is a great place for children, with crafts, storytelling, and the chance to meet their first sheep!

​

For more information about the Sheep & Wool Showcase, or for other questions about Clermont, please call (518) 537-4240 or contact Deanna.Blakeley@parks.ny.gov.

events.title: Author Talk & Book Signing with Kiersten Marcil
events.description:
Kiersten hails from New England and is proud of her family’s deep-rooted history in our nation’s foundation. She is a Mayflower descendant of Governor William Bradford (c. Mar. 19, 1590 – May 09, 1657) and great-granddaughter (several generations removed, of course) of Peter Minuit (btn 1580 & 1585 – Aug. 5, 1638). The latter was the Director of the Dutch North American Colony (1626 – 1631), which was part of the Dutch East India Company, and is credited with the so-called “purchase” of Manhattan Island. Her ancestors braved unknown dangers and a foreign world while creating treaties with the local peoples that survived for years under their leadership.

 Kiersten currently lives in upstate New York. Growing up, the Battlefields of Saratoga were her backyard and tourist sites like Fort Ticonderoga were an easy day trip, and she couldn’t have cared less back then.  It wasn’t until she journeyed into adulthood that Kiersten grew to love books, and even then, not until she became a museum educator, that she discovered the fascinating world of history. Tasked with bringing the past alive, she realized that behind every boring fact in textbooks were real people who lived and loved and dreamed and suffered losses and disappointments and helped shape the path on which we all journey. That realization turned the dreaded task of research into a compelling hobby of investigation.

 At present, she serves as the chair of the Schenectady Celebrates 250! committee for the Schenectady County Historical Society. So, if you’re looking for Kiersten, you will find her with her nose in a book or exploring another historical site or writing her next novel.

 Please note: This event is free, but due to space limitations, visitors must register in advance. Complimentary light refreshments will be available.

events.title: Back to the 80’s
events.description:
Throw it Back to the 80’s with us at The Tin Barn!
Get ready for a totally rad night of 80’s hits with DJ Bump spinning the classics! 


 Doors Open EARLY at 3PM

 Dig into those closets for that 80’s garb — prize for best dressed!
So bring your friends and your best dance moves — we will see you on the dance floor!

events.title: A Place in the World: Documentary Screening & Conversation
events.description:
Place in the World (2025, dir. David Shayne, 59min) traces the extraordinary sixty-year career of British sculptor Garth Evans (b. 1934), charting his path from working class roots to international acclaim.

At the center of the film is Untitled Sculpture, the monumental steel work Evans installed in Cardiff in 1972 as part of a nationwide public art program. Disguised and armed with a tape recorder, he stood beside the piece to capture the public’s candid reactions. With humor and honesty, the film explores themes of class, memory, and the enduring role of public art.

Followed by a conversation with Garth Evans and special guests, and screened in conjunction with Garth Evans: Watercolors.

A Place in the World (2025); Director, David Shayne; Executive Producers, West City Films; Producer, Leila Philip. Made by Pulpit Rock Films.

Tickets:
Free; reservations recommended

events.title: LIVE: Vicki “Turgeon Takes Requests”
events.description:
As a multi-instrumentalist, I infuse every note with uniqueness, passionately entertaining and authentically connecting with the audience, while staying anchored in deep respect for the music and patrons.
Taking requests is my favorite things about playing solo, so bring on your ideas, I’ll bring the courage to try new stuff!

Come out to Chatham Brewing with your friends and let’s have a fun night!!

events.title: LIVE: The Little Zippers: Lucia & Levi
events.description:
April showers bring … flowers and MUSIC! Shake off the clouds with blues and reggae from LUCIA & LEVI on Saturday.

While you’re here, put a little “spring” in your step with one of our new releases — TACONIC SUNDOWN rye lager and RJ JR light lager — and raise a glass to the new season!

events.title: LIVE: Spottiswoode & Kenny White
events.description:
Good friends Spottiswoode and Kenny White share the stage and trade songs for two sets, often accompanying each other. A virtuoso pianist, Kenny draws from a deep lyrical well—tender, hilarious, explosive. Spottiswoode will play tunes from his brand new album, “It Wasn’t In The Script,” which is a meditation on the joys and humiliations of fatherhood and getting older. No show is ever the same with these two songsmiths—they push each other forward into uncharted territory and with the help of the audience, they weave a narrative that’s bigger than both of them.

Spottiswoode is an award-winning bandleader, singer-songwriter, and scriptwriter. For the past two and a half decades, the Anglo-American has been the frontman of Spottiswoode & His Enemies, a septet that has become a New York institution. The New Yorker refers to him as a “genius” and “downtown ringleader.” With the band, he has released seven acclaimed records, performed numerous Manhattan residencies, and toured extensively—from SXSW and Lille Europe to Lincoln Center. He looks forward to touring with his new album in Europe and the United States for much of the rest of 2026.

Spottiswoode’s rock musical, “Between The Angel And The Old Kent Road,” was just workshopped in January and presented as a staged reading to potential investors at the Seven Dials Playhouse in London. His feature film screenplay, “Either Side Of Midnight,” was recently shot in New York City and helmed by James Bond director, Roger Spottiswoode (remarkably, no relation!).

Kenny White comes to the world of the touring singer-songwriter following a long and successful musical journey. An accomplished pianist, who also performs on guitar, he has now become more known for his clever lyrics. “Long List of Priors,” his aptly named current album, is the latest collection of original songs: snapshots of the human condition. White recently completed an 18-month tour performing his original songs while traveling as the opener for Stephen Stills & Judy Collins, and has toured for many years with Cheryl Wheeler. Audiences often comment that a Kenny White concert moves them to laughter and tears—sometimes within the same song. “If Mark Twain could sing and play piano like a virtuoso, his name would be Kenny White,” says Stu Kuby of The Strand THudson Falls, NY).

events.title: Earth Day & Robinson Forest Dedication
events.description:
Come out and celebrate with us!!

List of activities/events….

10:30am-1pm Germantown Parsonage, 52 Maple Ave /// Germantown History Tours, Bonfire, Sustainability Information, Refreshments courtesy of Cups Coffee and C.Cassis
10:30am Palatine Park Trail, meet behind Kellner Activity Building, 50 Palatine Park Rd /// Family Nature Walk courtesy of the Germantown Recreation Committee and Germantown Library
11:00am Germantown Parsonage, 52 Maple Ave /// Forest Opening Ceremony (Library Hover Room Rain Location)
11:30-1 Parsonage and adjacent Robinson Forest /// Guided Botany Tours
12-1:00 Germantown Library Hover Room, 31 Palatine Park Rd /// History of Theodore Robinson with town historian, Thomas Shannon
The Theodore Robinson Community Forest was funded in part by NYSDEC’s Community Forest Conservation Grant Program through the NYS Environmental Protection Fund.
Columbia County Soil and Water will donate a tree to be planted on Earth Day in each town in honor of the
United States Semiquincentennial. Germantown will be planting a Serviceberry tree.

events.title: Hudson Children’s Book Festival
events.description:
Our poster for this year’s event is AMAZING!! This gorgeous art was created by the talented Isabel Roxas. Meet her along with 75+ other incredible authors and illustrators at the 15th annual festival!

The Hudson Children’s Book Festival, established in 2009, is held each year in the historic city of Hudson, NY, and strives to create, nurture, and sustain a culture of literacy in partnership with our community and schools. This free, public event fosters a love of reading as families meet and greet world-class creators of books for children of all ages.

events.title: Chatham…In Our Own Words
events.description:
Stop the clock and enjoy this journey celebrating the Village of Chatham!

Personal interviews with community members that share what Chatham was like over the years. Sharing history and stories over the past 100 years.

events.title: Celebrate Frederic Church’s 200th Birthday!
events.description:
Enjoy free access to Olana’s historic house, fun giveaways, art making and nature activities, musical performances, and special birthday treats!

All Day

  • Free access to Olana’s historic interiors and free landscape tours
  • Art and nature activities for all ages offered by 4-H, Cornell Cooperative Extension’s Master Gardeners, Hawthorne Valley, and The Sylvia Center
  • Refreshments for purchase by local food vendors
  • Visits with friendly farm animals
  • Free photobooth
  • Fun free giveaways including Stewart’s Ice Cream, Bjorn Qorn, and Hudson Valley Seed Co. packets while supplies last!

1:30pm – Mariachi Music by Trovodores de América on the East Lawn

2:30pm – Middle Eastern Music by El Asdiqaa on the East Lawn

3:30pm – Ecuadorian Folk Music and Dancing by Andes Manta on the East Lawn

5:00pm – Book Talk with Frederic Church biographer, Victoria Johnson in the Frederic Church Center for Art & Landscape.  The day will conclude with a presentation by Pulitzer Prize nominated author Victoria Johnson whose biography of Frederic Church, Glorious Country, hits shelves on May 5. This book talk will be hosted in the Frederic Church Center for Art & Landscape and will highlight how Church brought the world to America, and America to the world. Books will be for sale and a book signing with the author will follow. FREE; no registration required. Seating will be provided first-come first served and space is limited.

events.title: Opening Reception: Pop-Up Exhibition, Shaker Outpost: Design, Commerce, and Culture
events.description:
Join us in celebrating the opening of Shaker Outpost: Design, Commerce, and Culture, featuring new work by renowned artist Maira Kalman in conversation with a selection of her favorite items from the Shaker Museum collection.

The General Store, a shop curated by Kalman that is complete with practical and poetic items for sale, will be open during the event.

Light refreshments and beverages will be served.

Entry is FREE with registration: Saturday, May 2, 2026: Opening Reception: Shaker Outpost – Shaker Museum

This exhibition will be on view through July 5

events.title: Equine Advocates Public Open Day
events.description:
Tour our 173-acre sanctuary and spend time with 80+ rescued horses, ponies, donkeys, and mules, most of whom were saved from slaughter, abuse, and/or neglect. We are also home to retired Military Working Horses. Learn their stories and see how much they enjoy their new lives! Feel free to bring a lunch to enjoy by the pond and picnic area.

Visit our website to learn more: https://www.equineadvocates.org/public-open-days-2026/

events.title: The Turning Point of the American Revolution: The Battles of Saratoga
events.description:
Eric Schnitzer, military historian at Saratoga National Park, will provide an overview of the military campaign which resulted in the Battles of Saratoga, leading to the first surrender of a British army in world history! Crowned the world’s “Battle of the Millenium” by the New York Times, Saratoga is generally hailed as the turning point of the Revolutionary War.

No charge to attend.

events.title: Ghent-Chatham Trivia Night!!
events.description:
The Columbia County Historical Society is excited to announce the first ever Ghent–Chatham Trivia Night at Chatham Brewing on May 7 at 7 p.m. Join Ghent Town Historian Gregg Berninger for a spirited evening of questions exploring the shared past and present of the two towns.
Participants can expect a fun, fast moving mix of local history, surprising facts, and friendly competition. Guaranteed fun—and guaranteed you’ll leave knowing more than you did when you arrived. Prizes will be awarded throughout the night.
Come test your knowledge, learn something new, and enjoy a great evening with neighbors at Chatham Brewing.

events.title: Opening Reception: Surface, Structure, String
events.description:
Surface, Structure, String celebrates the remarkable diversity of textile practices throughout our region. Featuring hand-dyed fabrics, conceptual embroidery, fiber-based sculpture, and immersive large-scale installations, this exhibition honors longstanding textile traditions while spotlighting artists who are redefining the medium as conceptually rigorous, socially engaged, and deeply connected to place. Curated by Richard Saja.

The exhibition features work by:

Margot Becker
Melissa Dadourian
Erika DeVries
Matthew Gilbert
Kat Howard
Hilary Gluck
Ellen Jouret-Epstein
Laleh Khorramian
Jess Ludwicki
Maeve McCool
Will McLeod
Portia Munson
Claudia Renfro
Richard Saja
Becca Van K
Anna Washburn

events.description: Valatie — let’s fill every seat and make it loud. Paul and Kacey brings the fire, the fun, and the kind of live music that turns Saturday night into a memory. Bring your friends, your neighbors, and anyone who loves a good time.

events.title: LIVE: The Upsides
events.description: The Upsides bring their NYC indie rock sound to Park Theater, blending jangle-pop melodies with driving, early-2000s guitar energy. The band is made up of brothers Matt and Dan Fullam alongside longtime friends Hart Mechlin, Pat Freeman, and Matt DaSilva. Their self-titled 2024 debut introduced a sound described as “irresistible 90s jangle pop,” pairing catchy songwriting with a modern edge. Now working on their second album, the band is expanding into a heavier, more dynamic direction while still holding onto their signature melodic core.

events.title: AKC Scent Trials
events.description:
Join us and our partner, Albany Obedience Club, as we host the AKC Scent Trials at the Museum. Many different breeds of dogs will be competing with their owners during this event. The dogs will be testing their ability to find designated scents inside and outside of the Museum building.
This event is included with regular admission to FASNY. Reservations are not required for this event.
PLEASE NOTE: ONLY CANINES REGISTERED FOR THIS EVENT WILL BE ALLOWED ON THE GROUNDS. Additionally, some galleries will be closed to accommodate these trials, but children’s activities, like discovery trucks and the Discovery Room will be open. The Museum will be open to the public, and visitors can watch portions of the trials for free with their paid admission. If you are a dog lover, this is certainly a rare treat, but if you are not canine inclined, or if you have children that are fearful of dogs, this is not a good weekend for you to visit the Museum. Thank you for your understanding, as we support emergency services with these trials.

events.title: Botanical Inks
events.description:
Space is limited ~ reserve your spot….Rain or Shine……Includes Lunch

Overview:
Spend the day exploring the fields and barn studio, where you’ll learn to create inks from freshly foraged plant materials. The workshop begins with a guided walk through the farm to gather seasonal botanicals, followed by a visit to the dye garden to harvest flowers. Back in the studio, you’ll transform your collected plants into a range of natural inks to take home. The day includes a farm-to-table lunch enjoyed outdoors in the fields.

What You Will Learn:
– How to forage for natural plant materials responsibly
– Practical methods for making artist-quality wild inks from botanicals
– Techniques for painting with natural inks on paper

Workshop Highlights:
– Foraging for wildflowers and botanicals in fields
– Working with botanicals to extract color
– Hands-on ink-making in the barn studio
– Farm-to-table lunch in the field (or greenhouse if rainy)
– Perfect for artists, nature enthusiasts and botany lovers, no experience necessary

Location:
– Jill’s barn studio in Chatham, NY on a serene 200-acre farm
– 25 minutes from Hudson, 25 minutes from Great Barrington, 2 hours north of Manhattan (access via Amtrak to Hudson)

What to Bring:
– Sketchbook for color swatching and note-taking
– Weather-appropriate layers and walking shoes or hiking boots
– Harvesting basket (if you have one)
– Hands ready to work, questions, and a belly ready to eat a delicious meal from the farm

Contact jill@farmandfieldny.com with questions or for recommendations on transportation and where to stay if needed.

No previous experience required, all skill levels and backgrounds are welcome.

Refund policy: Refunds are not available due to the long-term planning involved in our workshops. However, we offer credits that can be applied toward a custom workshop or a future workshop date.

events.title: Mom Fest!!
events.description: The Village of Valatie will be celebrating MOMS with street vendors, yummy treats, food trucks, local crafters, live music by The Beaks & More!!

events.title: Forage Fest: A Wild Food Gathering
events.description:
Where the land comes to the table. Foraged, cooked, and shared.

A Five-stop chef tasting experience featuring some of the Hudson Valley’s most celebrated kitchens

Talks and a panel discussion with foragers, farmers, and food writers on sustainable foraging and what it means to eat from the land.

Foraged cocktails, a curated maker market, and live music throughout the afternoon.

This is a full experience. Plan to stay, explore and eat well.

events.title: LIVE: Reed Foehl with special guest Hiroya Tsukamoto
events.description:
Reed Foehl Trio brings an evening of deeply rooted folk songwriting to Park Theater, joined by special guest Hiroya Tsukamoto. A Grammy-nominated artist, Foehl is known for weathered, honest songs that explore love, loss, and resilience, shaped by years on the road and a catalog of acclaimed releases. His music blends cinematic arrangements with a raw, lived-in voice.
Opening the night, Hiroya Tsukamoto delivers a masterful fingerstyle guitar performance, combining intricate playing with storytelling drawn from his life and travels, creating a thoughtful and immersive experience.

events.description:
Join us and our partner, Albany Obedience Club, as we host the AKC Scent Trials at the Museum. Many different breeds of dogs will be competing with their owners during this event. The dogs will be testing their ability to find designated scents inside and outside of the Museum building.
This event is included with regular admission to FASNY. Reservations are not required for this event.
PLEASE NOTE: ONLY CANINES REGISTERED FOR THIS EVENT WILL BE ALLOWED ON THE GROUNDS. Additionally, some galleries will be closed to accommodate these trials, but children’s activities, like discovery trucks and the Discovery Room will be open. The Museum will be open to the public, and visitors can watch portions of the trials for free with their paid admission. If you are a dog lover, this is certainly a rare treat, but if you are not canine inclined, or if you have children that are fearful of dogs, this is not a good weekend for you to visit the Museum. events.title: Spring Migration Bird Walk
events.description:
Join Columbia Land Conservancy (CLC) for a guided birding walk at Drowned Lands during peak migration season! Led by experienced birder Spencer Crawford, participants will learn about the migratory birds moving into and through the region.

What’s involved: This event involved walking about one mile on narrow packed earth trail over the Wetlands trail and over a portion of gravel road. There will be frequent stops to identify birds.

What to bring: Please bring a water bottle and wear weather appropriate clothing and footwear. Bringing a camera and/or binoculars is recommended for this program! CLC will have binoculars available to borrow.

What not to bring: Please, no dogs.

This program is weather-dependent! In the event that the weather on May 16 does not cooperate, it will be re-scheduled for May 23. Please be sure to check your email the day of the event.

events.title: Super Saturday ~ Make May Balloon Blowout
events.description:
Step up to the balloon bar, pick your colors and make a bodacious balloon creation. Choose from three spring-inspired projects. Do one, do them all, or go freestyle! Next, get your body moving as we play the balloon version of Hungry, Hungry Hippos. Can you pick up the most balloons and blow away the competition? Or are you the bouncy, trouncey type that can bounce your balloon on the paddle for the longest time during our Floating Frenzy Game? Did we mention there will be party music and colorful lighting to make this the balloon blowout party of the year? Come create and hang with us at the Museum during this Maker May event!

All activities are FREE and will be indoors. Free admission to the Museum will also be offered to all who attend this special event. Reservations are not required for this event.

events.title: Dinner & Comedy Show
events.description:
Join us for an unforgettable Dinner & Comedy Show at Catamount Mountain Resort

This adults-only evening of great food, drinks, and top-tier stand-up comedy. This is one of our most popular events of the year and sold out last season, so early ticket purchase is strongly recommended.

Headlining the night is Mike Finoia, a regular performer on Comedy Central, known for his sharp storytelling, high-energy delivery, and nationally recognized stand-up. Mike will be joined by two fellow comedians, making this a full, laugh-packed lineup from start to finish.

Your evening begins with a relaxed arrival and cocktails, followed by a buffet dinner, then settle in for the comedy show as the laughs kick off at 7:15PM. Enjoy a cash bar with table service, general seating, and a lively, social atmosphere designed for a great night out.

events.title: LIVE: Unfinish’d Bizness
events.description: Come out for some GREAT LIVE MUSIC and relax with some amazing food!!

events.title: “Spring Into Summer” Market
events.description: Local Makers, fun activities, food truck, horse drawn wagon rides and face painting!!! Great day of Family Fun!!

events.title: Firearms of the American Revolution
events.description:
For more than 30 years, Ron Toelke has participated in historic reenactments, learning the use of such firearms as the British Brown Bess, various French muskets and other period flintlock weapons. He helped form a Revolutionary War reenactment group of the 7th Albany militia, and later, units of the Continental First New York Regiment.

Several reproduction flintlock firearms will be demonstrated along with the necessary accouterments and other items needed to operate these weapons. There will be no live firing.

No charge to attend.

events.title: Land Camp: Pond Planting & Riparian Restoration
events.description:
The popular series is back for its third year!

Land Camp invites participants to learn about and try land management techniques in a supportive environment so that they can feel comfortable implementing them at home. This series of workshops is ideal for those already actively managing their land, those who would like to start, and those interested in learning more about how CLC manages the properties we care for.

Join CLC for a primer on using native species plantings to build riparian buffers around ponds. Dylan Cipkowski, Executive Director of the Columbia County Soil and Water District, will lead attendees through the identification and collection of certain native species plants such as elderberry and willow, that can be planted near ponds and other wetlands to improve water quality and reduce pressure from invasive species.

What’s involved: This event will include walking approximately a half mile of trail from the parking lot along the Red Fox and Pond Loop Trails. The trails are maintained but may include roots and rocky terrain. At times, the group may walk off-trail along the pond’s edge. Additional trail information can be found here.

Packing list: Water, weather-appropriate clothing and footwear, and a way to take notes (i.e. notebook, smartphone).

What not to bring: Please, no dogs.

events.title: LIVE: The Wallflowers
events.description:
The Wallflowers ~ Bringing Down The Horse

30th Anniversary Tour

Presented by The Bowery Presents, Higher Ground and Sister Midnight

events.title: SuperCar Races and Mopars In Motion’s New Vs Old Track Day
events.description:
SuperCar Racers, Mopars In Motion Members, and All Vintage and Modern Musclecar Owners, You’re Invited to the 14th Annual SuperCar Races American MuscleCar Racing New vs Old Event, Saturday May 23, 2026 at Lebanon Valley Dragway!!!
Think your new Hemi can hang with the original Elephant? Want to see if that Coyote Powered Mustang can catch a Road Runner? Think your Camaro can hang with an Old School Nova? Well this is the event you want to attend to try to answer those questions.
Come out to Lebanon Valley Dragway for a day filled with New vs Old Match Races. This is open to all that want to come out and have a fun day of racing with SuperCars and Mopars In Motion.
No cut off year, this is a New vs Old event. You will get up to three qualifying runs and then we break out into three rounds of match race madness.
Gate and Tech opens at 8am, Racing begins at 9am. We plan on having two to three qualifiers then we will pair up for a two out of three match race. New vs Old. Winner of each match race will receive a class winner sticker and Bragging Rights.
Trophies to Top New and Top Old Cars.
When you get to the track look for SuperCars truck and trailer with the SuperCar Banner. We will pit the complete group together behind the main grand stand.
We do a Group Lunch, and bring a grill. If you’d like to participate, bring something to share- Water, soda, Hot dogs, burgers, pasta salad, dessert, chips, etc. We will have plates, condiments, plasticware.
If you have any questions, visit SuperCarRaces.com, or contact Ralph Barbagallo 860 416- 1 2 2 8.

events.title: Hudson-Berkshire Annual Wine & Food Festival
events.description:
The  Spring Wine and Food Festival May 23rd-24th, 2026 at the Columbia County Fairgrounds, Chatham ,NY The festival  features beverage trail members plus other regional wineries, distilleries and cideries. 
There is also wonderful farm producers sampling and selling their delicious gourmet cheeses, baked goods, jams, jellies and so much more! Throughout the weekend there will be special seminars by some of the region’s foremost experts in the craft beverage industry.

Tasting Ticket – includes admission, souvenir tasting glass and all the wines, ciders and spirits you care to taste for one price.

One Day Tasting –  Online Tickets Available Here $20. 
Weekend Pass  Now Available Online   $25.00
$25.00  Day of  at the Gate  

General Admission – Non tasting Tickets- $10.
Children 12 and Under are free.

Tickets will be available at the gate.   Please use entrance on Rt. 66.​
